name: leios_duplicate_block_offer
description: The server sends two consecutive block_offer notifications for the identical EB point followed by three votes for that EB. In a real network a node may receive the same EB offer from multiple peers almost simultaneously; the LeiosNotify stream from a single peer can also retransmit an offer if state is reset. A conforming node must treat block_offer as idempotent: the second offer for an EB already in its pending-fetch table must not trigger a second fetch request, must not double-count the EB in any internal structure, and must not corrupt state. The subsequent votes must still be accumulated normally with the correct per-EB tally of 3. A buggy node might enter a broken state (e.g., double-allocated fetch slot, reference count mismatch) that causes a panic or stale entry when the EB is later resolved.
